reasoning: All three candidates pass the mechanical gate and my empirical probes for the core game (scripted win and loss, ASCII purity over a whole game including end screens, emoji non-ASCII, invalid input not consuming water, weeds rendered as a standalone count not a fraction of water, SPEC section 9 display order, repeated-letter words, EOF and KeyboardInterrupt clean exit 0). C is the clear adopt: it is the only candidate that honors the .code-tips.md MSG_* contract (tests assert against game.MSG_CORRECT/MSG_WRONG and a shared GOODBYE constant rather than re-typed literals), exposes a public growth_stage() that is unit-tested directly (including the win-beats-loss-at-zero-water invariant), reuses the engine's is_won/is_lost predicates instead of re-deriving win/loss from water, keeps emoji glyphs as \U escapes so the source itself is ASCII-safe, handles EOF and KeyboardInterrupt in one clean handler, and adds a captioned Plant line that is the most accessible growth-stage rendering. A has a real defect (argparse SystemExit is swallowed, so usage errors exit 0 instead of 2) and the weakest tests (its 'win' test never forces or asserts an actual win). B is solid and argparse-correct but its title glyph is static, one win test is effectively a no-op, and it ignores the MSG_* contract. Adopt C as-is; optionally backfill C with a couple of B's extra render-substring tests, but no synthesis is required.
